name: Cyrus Allen
headline: Deep dynasty flier buried behind Kansas City's crowded top three
body:
Allen arrives in Kansas City as a fifth-round rookie out of Cincinnati, where he stacked 51 grabs for 674 yards and 13 scores across 13 games in 2025. That touchdown production hints at real upside, but the path to volume is steep: Rashee Rice and Xavier Worthy are entrenched, with Tyquan Thornton, Jalen Royals and Allen scrapping over the leftover snaps behind them. He profiles as a target-earner with genuine separation skills and positional flexibility, the kind of trait set that can force its way onto the field if camp breaks right.

The market treats him accordingly. He sits near 283rd in early ADP and inside WR144 in redraft rankings, strictly a late-round dart in deep formats. Dynasty is where the appeal lives, as a Round 3 rookie-draft cost for a player on a high-powered offense. Projections of roughly 6 catches and 80 yards on the season underscore the reality: monitor training camp, but do not draft him expecting standalone value.
